instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: Curly brackets surround all newly declared information, keeping it hidden from code outside of the curly brackets.

runs.text: Curly brackets surround all newly declared information, keeping it hidden from code outside of the curly brackets.

text_blocks.text: Darker shades can see lighter shades, but not vice versa

runs.text: Darker shades can see lighter shades, but not vice versa

text_blocks.text: A Switch-Case statement is like a long if, else-if, else block concerning only one variable and several possible discrete values (not ranges).

runs.text: is like a long if, else-if, else block concerning only one variable and several possible discrete values (not ranges).

text_blocks.text: This increases readability, but they act similarly.

runs.text: This increases readability, but they act similarly.

text_blocks.text: Works for byte, char, short, int, and String.  (Any others out of scope)
For every case possibility, you need a break statement, unless you wanted to combine several cases together.

text_blocks.text: I need a function that returns the first 100 terms of the harmonic series.

runs.text: I need a function that returns the first 100 terms of the harmonic series.

text_blocks.text: Using 100 variables as a list is not scalable!  What if we need more?  What if I change the names?  What if I wanted a different series?

runs.text: Using 100 variables as a list is not scalable!  What if we need more?  What if I change the names?  What if I wanted a different series?

text_blocks.text: Arrays are ordered lists of things depending on the type you specify

text_blocks.text: All operations for int are allowed for an individual element
